revision.text:
[[ja:十五社神社]] [[ja:十五社神社]] [[ja:十五社神社]] [[ja:十五社神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Jūgosha Shrine (十五社神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Juugosya jinja ooga.jpg | a11ress = Ōga 2281, Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.545111|N|136.759611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tokotachi|qq=}} and 15 other enshrined deities (20 pillars in total) | classification = Former Gō-sha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119929592}} | established = Traditional date: {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|qid=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō (era)|1=Tenchō}} 3 (826 CE) | architecture = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with hinoki bark roof | festival = First Sunday in April | rituals = Yu-barai | map = {{Switcher |{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=180|frame-height=180|type=point|frame-latitude=35.529|frame-longitude=136.762}} |Show Wikimedia maps |{{location map|Japan Gifu|float=center|width=180}} |Show Gifu Prefecture map }} | location = {{ill|Yamagata|qid=Q773311|1=Yamagata, Gifu}} | deity = {{ill|Amenotokotachi|qid=Q10939047|1=Ame-no-Tokotachi}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Jūgosha Shrine''' (十五社神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Yamagata, Gifu|en|Yamagata, Gifu|qid=Q773311|lt=Yamagata City|1=Yamagata, Gifu}}, Gifu Prefecture. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} and was classified as a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119929592}} under Gifu Prefecture’s unique shrine classification system. It was established in {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|qid=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō (era)|1=Tenchō}} 3 (826 CE) by {{ill|Haruhara Ioe|ja|春原五百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruhara Ioe|1=Haruhara no Ioe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11513416}}, who constructed the shrine to enshrine the deity known as "Amatsukami Daimyōjin." The shrine enshrines deities from the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|qid=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}}, the divine lineage of Japan’s emperors, and the tutelary deities of the Genji clan, offering blessings for national security, prosperity of the imperial family, and familial safety. The shrine was revered by the {{ill|Toki clan|en|Toki clan|qid=Q687635|lt=Toki clan|1=Toki clan}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}} as their tutelary deity. The shrine’s main hall, rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|en|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|qid=Q313745|lt=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|1=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi}}, is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Property. The {{ill|Shokudani stone|ja|笏谷石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokudani stone|1=Shakudani Stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11599510}} komainu donated by the Toki clan are recognized as a Yamagata City Important Cultural Property. == Enshrined Deities == 15 deities, enshrined as 20 pillars: * {{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tokotachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|天神七代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神世七代}}Deities such as {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuninotokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} are counted in pairs, representing one "generation" or "seat." ''' {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuninotokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} ''' {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Toyokumunu|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumunu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4387158}} ''' {{ill|Uhijini|ja|埿土煮尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uhijini|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウヒヂニ・スヒヂニ|1=Uhijini and Suhijini|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9090923}} and {{ill|Suhijini|ja|沙土煮尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suhijini|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウヒヂニ・スヒヂニ|1=Uhijini and Suhijini|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9090923}} ''' {{ill|Ōtonoji|ja|大戸之道尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtonoji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|1=Otonoji and Otonobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11073597}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大苫辺尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオトノヂ・オオトノベ}} ''' {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Omotaru|ja|面足尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ}} and {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Kashikone|ja|惶根尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ}} ''' {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}} * {{ill|Chijin-godai|ja|地神五代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijin-godai|1=Five Generations of Earth Deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18337308}} ''' {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大紳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q462555}} ''' {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} ''' {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} ''' {{ill|Ugaya Fukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugaya Fukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神日本磐余彦尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇|1=Emperor Jimmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q200188}} * {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}Seimu and Ōjin are also paired as one "seat." == History == According to legend, in May of {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|qid=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō (era)|1=Tenchō}} 3 (826 CE), amid fierce lightning and hail, the deity {{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tokotachi|qq=}} and 17 other deities from the Kamiyonanayo and Chijin-godai descended, instructing the construction of a shrine for national protection before departing. Some theories link the shrine to the "Seishin Myōjin" mentioned in the {{ill|Mino no Kuni Shinsen Meishocho|ja|国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino no Kuni Shinsen Meishocho|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420490}}, listed among the 12 shrines in {{ill|Yamagata District, Hiroshima|en|Yamagata District, Hiroshima|qid=Q1155572|lt=Yamagata District, Gifu|1=Yamagata District, Hiroshima}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, {{ill|Toki Yorinari|en|Toki Yorinari|qid=Q837179|lt=Toki Yorie|1=Toki Yorinari}} brought the spirit of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qid=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} to the shrine, merging it and renaming it "Jūgosha Daigongen." It became the clan deity of the Toki family. In {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 14 (1701), the main hall was rebuilt. Later, during the {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|qid=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} era (1804–1818), the shrine was renamed "Jūgosha Daijingū." In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), it was renamed to its current name and designated a village shrine. It was promoted to a gōsha in 1944. == Rituals == ;Yu-barai (Ritual Purification by Boiling Water) :This ritual is said to be a remnant of ancient trial-by-boiling-water {{ill|Kugatachi|en|Kugatachi|qid=Q11581453|lt=Kuga no Utagai|1=Kuchiyose}}. A large cauldron is set over a fire, and the {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba|en|Ōharae no Kotoba|qid=Q11438317|lt=Ōharai Norito|1=Ōharae no Kotoba}} is recited a thousand times. == Cultural Properties == Gifu Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties: * Main Hall{{Cite web|title=Jūgosha Shrine|url=https://www.city.yamagata.gifu.jp/site/yamanavi/1132.html|publisher=Yamagata City|accessdate=2023-12-04}} - Built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style with a hinoki bark roof, reconstructed in Genroku 14 (1701). Yamagata City Designated Cultural Properties: * {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|qid=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}} - Donated in {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21096812}} 9 (1540) by the Toki clan, carved from {{ill|Shokudani stone|ja|笏谷石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokudani stone|1=Shakudani Stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11599510}}. City-Designated Natural Monuments: * Henmi Cedar - A cedar tree with a circumference of 5.2 meters. Legend states it was planted by {{ill|Henmi Shigekazu|ja|逸見重氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Henmi Shigekazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Transportation == * From the Hā Bus line Ōga route, alight at "Shikokuyama Kaori no Mori Kōen" bus stop, then walk for 15 minutes. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Association of Shinto Shrines Research Department, ''Jinja Meikan,'' Association of Shinto Shrines, 1963. == External Links == * [http://www.city.gifu-yamagata.lg.jp/sight/rekishi/13.html Jūgosha Shrine] (Yamagata City official website) {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|十五社神社|version=98925505|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:十五社神社]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Jūgoshajinja (十五社神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Juugosya jinja ooga.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall of Jūgoshajinja | location = Ōga 2281, Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|32|42.4|N|136|45|34.6|E|region:JP-21_scale:25000|display=it}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之常立神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tokotachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10939047}} and 15 seats, 20 kami | type = Former Gō-sha, Gifu Prefectural Kanpeisha | established = Traditional date: Tenchō 3 (826 CE) | architecture = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with cypress bark roof | festival = 1st Sunday in April | rituals = {{ill|Yubarai|ja|湯祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubarai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | map = {{Switcher |{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=pp|frame-align=center|frame-width=180|frame-height=180|type=point|frame-latitude=35.529|frame-longitude=136.762}} |Show Wikimedia maps |{{location map|Japan Gifu|float=center|width=180}} |Show Gifu Prefecture map }} }} '''Jūgoshajinja''' (十五社神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto shrine}} shrine located in {{ill|Yamagata, Gifu|en|Yamagata, Gifu|qid=Q773311|lt=Yamagata City (Gifu)|1=Yamagata, Gifu}}, Gifu Prefecture. It was designated as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Rank system and holds the unique Gifu Prefectural Rank of Kanpeisha. The shrine was established in Tenchō 3 (826 CE) by Haruhara Ioe, who built the shrine to enshrine the deity {{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tokotachi|qq=}}. The shrine venerates deities connected to the Japanese imperial lineage, from the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|qid=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}} to the first emperor, reflecting divine virtues of national stability, royal prosperity, and familial safety. During the {{ill|Sengoku Period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku Period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}, the shrine was revered as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Toki clan|en|Toki clan|qid=Q687635|lt=Toki clan|1=Toki clan}}. Its main hall, reconstructed under the orders of Tokugawa Tsunayoshi, is designated a Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property, and the shrine's stone guardian lions, donated by the Toki clan, are designated as cultural assets by Yamagata City. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines 15 seats and 20 kami: * {{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之常立神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tokotachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10939047}} * {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|天神七代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神世七代}}Paired deities such as Kuninotokotachi and Kuni-no-sazuchi-no-mikoto are treated as two kami for one seat. ** {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuninotokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} ** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ** {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}} ** {{ill|Uhijini and Suhijini|lt=Uhijini and Suhijini|lt=Uhiji|ja|埿土煮尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uhiji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウヒヂニ・スヒヂニ}} and {{ill|Uhijini and Suhijini|lt=Uhijini and Suhijini|lt=Suhiji|ja|沙土煮尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suhiji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウヒヂニ・スヒヂニ}} ** {{ill|Otonoji and Otonobe|lt=Otonoji and Otonobe|lt=Ōtonomichino-Mikoto|ja|大戸之道尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtonomichino-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオトノヂ・オオトノベ}} and {{ill|Otonoji and Otonobe|lt=Otonoji and Otonobe|lt=Ōtomabe|ja|大苫辺尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomabe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオトノヂ・オオトノベ}} ** {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Omotaru|ja|面足尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ}} and {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Kashikone|ja|惶根尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ}} ** {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}} * {{ill|Five Generations of Earth Deities|lt=Five Generations of Earth Deities|lt=Chijin Godai|ja|地神五代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijin Godai|qq=}} ** {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q462555}} ** {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} ** {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}} ** {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}Emperor Seimu and Emperor Ōjin are counted as one seat. == History == According to tradition, in May of Tenchō 3 (826 CE), a fierce storm accompanied by thunder and hail brought down the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tokotachi|qq=}} and other members of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|qid=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}} and {{ill|Five Generations of Earth Deities|lt=Five Generations of Earth Deities|lt=Chijin Godai|ja|地神五代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijin Godai|qq=}}. These deities instructed the establishment of a shrine for the nation’s protection before disappearing. It is believed that this shrine corresponds to "Shōshi Myōjin" (正四位下 裁主明神) listed in the {{ill|Mino Province's Shrine Ledger|ja|美濃国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province's Shrine Ledger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the Toki clan, ruling from Ōga Castle, invited the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qid=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} deity to the shrine and renamed it "Jūgoshajinja." In {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 14 (1701 CE), the main hall was reconstructed, and in {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|qid=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} 9 (1812 CE), the shrine was renamed "Jūgosha Daijingu" by Prince Iwakura Tomosue. In 1872, it was renamed to its current name and designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, later upgraded to a Gōsha in 1944. == Rituals == ; Yubarai (湯祓) : This ancient purification ritual is a vestige of {{ill|Kugatachi|en|Kugatachi|qid=Q11581453|lt=Kuchiyose|1=Kuchiyose}}, in which boiling water was used for divine oaths. A large cauldron is heated while the {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba|en|Ōharae no Kotoba|qid=Q11438317|lt=Ōharae Kotoba|1=Ōharae no Kotoba}} is chanted 1,000 times. == Cultural Properties == * '''Prefectural Important Cultural Property''' ''' Main Hall - {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, cypress bark roof, reconstructed in Genroku 14 (1701 CE).{{Cite web|date=2023-12-04 |url=https://www.city.yamagata.gifu.jp/site/yamanavi/pp32.html |title=Jūgoshajinja |publisher=Yamagata City |accessdate=2023-12-04}} * '''Municipal Cultural Properties''' ''' Stone Guardian Lions - Donated by the Toki clan in {{ill|Tenmon|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon|qq=}} 9 (1540 CE). * '''Municipal Natural Monument''' ''' Henmi Cedar - A cedar tree with a girth of 5.2 meters, said to have been planted by Henmi Shigeuji after his achievements in the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|qid=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}}. == Access == * {{ill|Harubasu|ja|ハーバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harubasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山県市自主運行バス|1=Harubasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11327306}} Ōga Line, get off at "Shikoku Yamakōrino Mori Kōen" stop, 15-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Jinja Meikan|ja|神社名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinja Meikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, compiled by the Shinto Shrine Association, 1963. == External Links == * [http://www.city.gifu-yamagata.lg.jp/sight/rekishi/13.html Jūgoshajinja] (Yamagata City Homepage) {{translated page|ja|十五社神社|version=98925505|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:十五社神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11404828}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamagata, Gifu]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Yamagata, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
